rationale.R1.2: The section makes non-trivial analytical claims — notably that self-consistency worsens calibration on long-horizon tasks due to systematic bias convergence, and that per-step uncertainty propagation avoids this. These mechanism attributions require logical scrutiny and could be load-bearing depending on the reviewer's intent.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are asserted (38% average ECE reduction, 47% on ToolBench, 0.12 Brier improvement, <1.5pp success-rate delta) without in-text figures or tables to ground them. Checking whether these numbers are internally consistent and plausibly supported by the described experimental protocol is plausibly load-bearing.

rationale.R1.7: Three benchmark names and three baseline methods are referenced without formal citations; peripherally relevant if citation completeness is in scope, but not clearly requested.
